title: Bisect, Reproducible Diagnosis, and Automated Regression Search
description: A nondeterministic performance regression lies within 2,400 commits, skips unbuildable revisions, depends on generated assets, and may have entered on a merged side branch. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible bisect harness and causal report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.
Define known good and bad endpoints, path limits, terms, first-parent choices, and ancestry validity before the first trial.
Build a hermetic bisect run harness with explicit exit meanings, timeouts, cleanup, logs, deterministic fixtures, and no unsafe repository code execution.
